findings.description: The document references 'Section 6' for security considerations, but no Section 6 exists anywhere in the document. The document has five named sections: Overview, Architecture Components, Performance Characteristics, Operational Notes, and Conclusions. The reference is a broken internal pointer.
findings.evidence: "Security considerations are documented in Section 6." — The document contains no Section 6 and no Security section.
findings.suggestion: Either add a Section 6 covering security considerations, or remove the forward reference and inline the essential security notes where relevant.

findings.description: The Overview states all pipeline stages run in parallel, but Stage 2 explicitly states it must complete before Stage 3 begins. This is a direct architectural contradiction that leaves the pipeline's execution model undefined for a final architecture brief. A final artifact must resolve this.
findings.evidence: Overview: "All pipeline stages run in parallel to maximize throughput." Stage 2: "Stage 2 must complete before Stage 3 begins."
findings.suggestion: Correct the Overview to accurately state the execution model. If stages 1 and 4 run independently/in parallel while stages 2 and 3 are sequential, say so explicitly.

findings.location: Stage 1 — Ingestion; Stage 2 — Transformation; Stage 3 — Delivery
findings.description: Three undefined components are referenced as architectural elements but never described: (1) the dead-letter queue (Stage 1), (2) the overflow queue (Stage 2), and (3) the reference database (Stage 2). For a final architecture brief, each component that appears in the architecture must be defined. These are load-bearing architectural elements with no specification.
findings.evidence: Stage 1: "logged to the dead-letter queue" — no definition. Stage 2: "via an overflow queue" — no definition. Stage 2: "customer metadata from the reference database" — no definition.
findings.suggestion: Add an Architecture Components subsection or appendix defining each supporting component: dead-letter queue (retention, consumer), overflow queue (trigger threshold, consumer), and reference database (type, access pattern, latency expectations).

findings.description: Authentication is mentioned as occurring but is not specified. For a final architecture brief, the authentication mechanism must be identified (e.g., API key, OAuth2, mTLS). The current text is a placeholder-level description.
findings.evidence: "Authentication is used to verify each request before processing begins." — No mechanism, credential type, or authority is named.
findings.suggestion: Specify the authentication mechanism: e.g., 'Requests are authenticated via HMAC-signed API keys included in the X-API-Key header.'

findings.description: The 'standard retry policy' is referenced as if defined elsewhere, but no such definition exists in this document and no external reference is provided. A final architecture brief must either define it or cite where it is defined.
findings.evidence: "Transformation applies the standard retry policy on transient failures." — No definition of 'standard retry policy' appears anywhere in the document.
findings.suggestion: Define the retry policy inline (e.g., exponential backoff with up to N attempts over T seconds) or cite the policy document by name and location.

findings.description: The claim that the in-memory delivery cache reduces latency by 99.9% is presented as a factual specification with no basis, source, or measurement conditions stated. This is an extraordinary quantitative claim that cannot be verified from the document. In a final architecture brief, unsubstantiated performance figures undermine the document's reliability.
findings.evidence: "The in-memory delivery cache reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9% compared to unbuffered writes."
findings.suggestion: Either provide the measurement basis (benchmark conditions, test environment, data volume) or restate as an approximation with appropriate hedging. If the figure is erroneous, correct it.

findings.description: The Operational Notes section references 'the Prometheus dashboard' with a definite article implying a specific known artifact, but the dashboard is never named, linked, or described. Minor completeness gap for a final artifact.
findings.evidence: "The pipeline and workflow are monitored by the on-call team via the Prometheus dashboard."
findings.suggestion: Name or link the dashboard (e.g., 'via the Meridian Pipeline Grafana dashboard at <url>') so operators can locate it.

notes.reexamine_result: Second pass confirmed all findings. No findings were added or removed during re-examination. The parallel-vs-sequential contradiction (STRUCTURAL-002) was identified on re-examination as a high-severity structural validity issue, not merely a consistency observation.
